Slot: CHEST
AC: 22
STR: +5 DEX: -2 WIS: +3
WT: 0.8 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG SHM BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Kylong Chestplate
Lore Item No Trade
Slot: CHEST
AC: 22
STR: +5 DEX: -2 WIS: +3
WT: 0.8 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR CLR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG SHM BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Kunark Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):70, 20, 20
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Ramulet
Lucy Entry By:Tiana
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 02:43:09
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 45

a hexbone skeleton
Skeletal Procurator

and
QuoteReply
# May 17 2007 at 2:32 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592774178472
16 posts
also drops from "an undertaker skeleton" roamer near drusella's room
▲ Page top
Value of Kylong
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2003 at 12:39 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591766778716
Scholar
42 posts
One additional thing that makes Kylong not quite as awful as some is its usefulness to iksar shadowknights (or warriors) who cannot wear Blood Ember, Sentient, or other forms of plate. Sebilite Scale looks slightly better, but Kylong isn't too far behind.

Considering, though, that a Netherbian Chitin (AC20 STR2) goes for under 50pp nowadays, I wouldn't pay lots of money for 2 more AC and a couple more stats.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Dec 06 2002 at 9:26 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is the biggest piece of junk I have encountered in HS and I hang out there quite a bit.
RE: Give me a break
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2003 at 5:33 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592247435153
497 posts
Understand that for the era when this came out, an AC of 22 with stats was great for some of these classes. Go ahead, what was better? Name something that (a) doesn't require more than 1 group to get and (b) existed when Kunark came out.

Trak drops better, but I guarantee you that I could drop this guy and the whole wing with a group that would drop dead before getting Trak to half. AFAIR, either naggy or vox drop better, but they're still tougher than this guy by far.

Nope, this is good loot for the era. I do wish that VI would go back and re-do a few more of the old zones soon to focus on making some of this stuff worth the effort NOW, but still... nothing beats the risk/reward of HS solo exp unless you can solo in PoP or The Hole. A necro can solo in HS as early as 51 if he's really good.
▲ Page top
crap
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2002 at 10:52 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757397175
217 posts
for the amount of effort it takes to get into HS and fight through it, this and most of the kylong armor are pretty ******. They are sort of comparable to the Twilight Sea Quest armor without the saves.

what a crock.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jul 02 2004 at 5:11 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) for the amount of effort it takes to get into HS ?????
RE: crap
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2004 at 11:18 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1625444777079
135 posts
Try looking at the DATE when that was posted was a different time then and HS was harder to enter. Would be nice if people would realize that just because they started playing last month does not make them experts. Should read more and speak less
▲ Page top
Tanks and Mana
QuoteReply
# Jun 16 2002 at 1:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591754967810
29 posts
Torylori, you are horribly misinformed. First off, your hybrid tanks (for sake of arguement I'm only going to count these as Paladins and Rangers ) need all the mana they can get and I'll tell you why:
Paladins - Stuns, Roots, Buffs when Cleric is LoM
Rangers - Snares/Roots, Harmony (if pulling), Buffs, Jolt/Flame Lick
The biggest problem we wisdom-hybrid tanks have is lack of sufficient downtime. While you caster's are sitting around medding, we are in the midst of combat keeping you alive. We don't have time to med. So again, we need all the mana we can get.
Oh, and I know some munchkin out there is going to want to get all high and mighty and flame me for calling Paladins and (especially) Rangers tanks. Don't bother. If you don't believe that either of these two classes can tank (yes, even post-50) then you also are misinformed.
▲ Page top
RE: Tanks and Mana
QuoteReply
# Jul 13 2002 at 4:44 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591769221310
63 posts
Bravo
As a Tank-Warrior
who has a Druid and a Ranger too I must say I look for mana when ever I play my ranger. A GOOD Ranger is welcome anywhere and a bad Warrior is LFG.
▲ Page top
ranger wearable
QuoteReply
# Jun 12 2002 at 5:19 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776206197
Scholar
16 posts
So rangers can wear this item, as can iksar, but shamen and rogues cant....well, that makes a hell of a lot of sense doesn't it? And what warrior 45 + would want it? As pathetic as my gear was at that level, I even had better.(unfortunately I'm still wearing most of it) :P Don't get me wrong, I dont begrudge the little treehuggers...err, rangers...their 22 ac bp. I just think that if it's going to be available to chain classes and iksar it should be consistent and be available to ALL chain classes. But what do I know, I'm just a big ignorant savage. :)

Beornegar Frostwolf
51 Champion
Order of the White Fang
Innoruuk
▲ Page top
RE: ranger wearable
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2002 at 8:35 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750044884
48 posts
I agree :( so right now Shaman's are left out (like normal) on wisdom items. I'm really getting sick of this.

Not to Slam my Meat shields ... but what the heck do they need Wisdom in the first place? They do not cast spells, after all they are TANKs.
▲ Page top
RE: ranger wearable
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2002 at 2:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758433518
Scholar
160 posts
Well, if ya want a real explanation I can't give it to you other than this: Since the Kylong were/are Iksar, chain-class armor IS plate type armor to them. Only typically plate wearing classes were on the list before. I mean perhaps even though it is chain, it is made in a style similar to plate while being physically made from chain links to allow cooling for us cold-blooded Iksar.

As to why shamans and rogues can't tolerate it...I don't know. Maybe the powers that be decided that Rangers needed another step in their armor uppgrade path, while Shaman and Rogues were deemed okay in that regard. Anyway, it's not very good armor for either class in any case IMO. Not enough WIS and no HP/Mana for a Shaman, and not enough AC, Str or HP for a Rogue to care. Much better stuff around for either class. Just my 2cp, but I think I make sense. Not every item is made for every class that COULD use it.
▲ Page top
w00t
QuoteReply
# Jun 12 2002 at 1:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776948404
11 posts
well, all of u who were upset that rng etc couldn't wear, and thought that they should be able to cuz pic makes it look like chain mail...

as of today, with the new patch, WE CAN! :-)

see...VI does listen sometimes
▲ Page top
why iksar can use
QuoteReply
# Oct 25 2001 at 6:34 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245749019
Scholar
35 posts
The Kylong were a mountain dwelling tribe of Iksar that strove for esoteric knowledge. They had vast libraries of ancient magical tomes that had been acquired from the Shissar sorcerers, and strove to understand and put the rituals documented in the tomes to practical use. When the Kunzar armies marched upon the Kylong they where distroyed
▲ Page top
Misleading Icon
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 10:48 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
It's a chestPLATE -- and the Classes list is exactly the list of Plate-wearing classess - no Shaman or Rogues either, for example -- so you're really only going on the the misleading icon.
▲ Page top
RE: Misleading Icon
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2002 at 8:26 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750044884
48 posts
Key Word is CHESTplate ... meaning Chain Classes.

Breastplate is Plate Classes.

Tunic is for Leather Classes.

Cause Shaman's I know can wear all the other pieces, so if this is Plate armor .. All pieces would not be wearable by Chain classes.
▲ Page top
RE: Misleading Icon
QuoteReply
# Sep 29 2001 at 9:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592756556801
13 posts
Funny, last I knew iksar were not a plate wearing race

# Feb 21 2002 at 10:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762323414

I've got a great idea...I have an iksar SK...this item seems like it should be plate as it's name suggests. I CAN WEAR IT. I'll come here and biotch about how i can wear it and how i shouldn't be able to! how good life is! /sarcasm off

Who cares? by the time you get in a group to get this NO DROP breastplate, you should have much better anyway.

Well I've personally never been so lame as to call people idiots while anonymous, but hey. It's no drop so it's worth nothing at all. If it was tradeable, it would rock.
